reviewer: hand-authored (Opus) — verified-runnable-first (real headless-Chrome inference on the exact transformers.js 3.7.5 CDN build against a byte-identical local mirror), then driven headless-Chrome page matrix (desktop 1280x800 + mobile 360x740 DPR3, screenshots read)

rubric.evidence: Verified runnable BEFORE building: loaded FinDIT-Studio/wav2vec2-large-xlsr-53-japanese-onnx via AutoModelForCTC on the transformers.js 3.7.5 CDN build in real headless Chrome (WASM), fed the FLEURS Japanese clip through the EXACT worker code path (architecture config borrowed from the base jonatasgrosman repo + subfolder:"" + dtype fp32 + manual wav2vec2 zero-mean/unit-variance normalisation + id→char map from the bundled ~2,300-entry vocab.json + hand-built greedy CTC decode) and got REAL logits dims [1, 605, 2341] and a coherent Japanese transcript (kanji+hiragana): '日本や約七線の島島がありその最弟は本週列頭で世界で七だ段目に大きいまとされています' (reference: '日本には約7,000の島々があり その最大は本州列島で 世界で7番目に大きい島とされています') — 日本 / 約 / 島 / 本(州→週) / 列(島→頭) / 世界 / 大きい / とされています all correctly recognised, with expected character-CTC homophone substitutions (週/州 both しゅう, 頭/島 both とう, 弟/大). Source loads the model in a Web Worker and does a real greedy CTC argmax→collapse — no canned result.
rubric.notes: The files were served from a LOCAL mirror of the identical HF repo (model.onnx downloaded whole from the HF CDN, byte-identical to production which fetches the same file) so the 1.27 GB fp32 load could complete inside the harness time budget; the code path, config, feature extraction and decode are identical to production. The FinDIT repo ships ONLY model.onnx (single self-contained fp32 file, ~1.27 GB, no external-data sidecar) + tokenizer.json — no config.json/preprocessor_config.json — handled + disclosed exactly as the built Spanish/Italian/Portuguese XLSR demos.

rubric.evidence: Output is a Japanese transcript string whose characters match the spoken audio (verified against the FLEURS reference). Real logits [1, 605, 2341] over the ~2,300-symbol character vocab spanning hiragana/katakana/kanji. 'See inside' renders the real per-frame argmax strip and the mechanical collapse (merge duplicates, drop blanks) into the transcript. Practical page derives real per-CHARACTER forced-alignment timings from the frame each character was emitted at (per-character is the natural unit — Japanese is written without word spaces).
rubric.notes: Expected shape (frames × ~2341-symbol vocab → collapsed text + per-character timings) is correct; kanji + hiragana + katakana all appear in real output.

rubric.evidence: conformance-derived: declares-model-id (FinDIT-Studio/wav2vec2-large-xlsr-53-japanese-onnx linked to card), declares-task (automatic-speech-recognition), declares-backend (WASM/WebGPU), declares-quantisation (fp32), inference-off-main-thread (worker.js). At-a-glance names the XLSR-53 architecture, the ~2,300-symbol three-script vocab, dtype fp32, AND honestly explains the non-standard load quirks: the single fp32 model.onnx at the repo root (loaded with subfolder:""), the missing config.json (architecture config borrowed from the base jonatasgrosman repo), the missing preprocessor_config.json (feature normalisation done in-worker), and the id→char map from the bundled vocab.json.
rubric.notes: Backend/dtype match what worker.js actually loads (device webgpu-if-adapter-else-wasm, dtype fp32, subfolder "", config from base repo). Honest, complete disclosure incl. the ~1.27 GB one-time download and the base-model Apache-2.0 license.

rubric.evidence: Driven fresh profile on all 5 pages: loader in 'download-required' with a single honest 'Download model (~1272 MB)' button — no silent transfer (verified overflow/console matrix; multi-model shows two honest buttons '~1272 MB' + '~120 MB'). Uses the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (backed by the version-agnostic model-cache), which auto-inits a cached-current version with only a Clear-cache button — the same lib verified on the other 212 built demos.
rubric.notes: Cold absent-state + no-silent-download driven directly. The warm cached auto-init path was not separately driven on the live CDN in this pass because the 1.27 GB fp32 download does not complete within the headless harness time budget in this environment (openQuestion) — the model load itself is proven via the byte-identical local-mirror run; the auto-init behaviour is the shared loader's, exercised by the rest of the catalogue.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region (role=status aria-live present), errors-on-the-page (.err status surface + Retry via loader). Transcribe shows 'Running the CTC model…' then 'Done.'; decode/mic failures set status .err with the message; a mic-unavailable fallback panel explains sample/upload alternatives. runs-without-webgpu-fallback: worker prefers a real WebGPU adapter and falls back to WASM. Driven pages: 0 console errors, 0 failed network requests across all 5.
rubric.notes: Progress/ready/error wired accessibly. Offline-cached reuse and a forced partial/evicted cache path were not separately driven this pass (openQuestion).

rubric.evidence: Main page: Record (mic), Upload, Japanese sample chip, Transcribe, audio <player> controls. Basics: upload + sample + Transcribe (+ per-frame strip). Practical: Transcribe & align → clickable per-CHARACTER chips that replay the exact time span (real <button>s with aria-labels). Wild: in-language (Japanese) vs out-of-language (English JFK) sample chips + Transcribe. Multi-model: two loaders + Transcribe & translate. Driven check: 0 unnamed buttons on every page. Primary buttons min-block-size:44px; chips 40px.
rubric.notes: controls-operable is manual-evidenced; a full keyboard-only traversal (tab order + visible focus across all 5 pages) is the remaining verification.

rubric.evidence: Before a run the transcript shows only an instructional placeholder and the see-inside panel shows an empty-state message; the loader shows the honest absent-model state on a fresh profile (download-required). The Japanese transcript that appears is produced by the on-device CTC run (logits [1,605,2341] observed in the local-mirror verification of the exact worker code path).
rubric.notes: No pre-baked result anywhere; the Wild page shows honest cross-lingual breakdown (Japanese-phonetic characters) on English input rather than hiding it.

rubric.evidence: conformance: images-have-alt (canvas waveform + strip carry aria-label/role=img; no bare content ), buttons-have-names (driven: 0 unnamed buttons on all 5 pages). Status uses role=status aria-live=polite; transcript/collapsed use aria-live=polite; Japanese regions marked lang="ja" (correct SR pronunciation + CJK font/line-break selection); per-character replay buttons have descriptive aria-labels.
rubric.notes: Strong baseline. A full screen-reader pass over the transcript and the per-frame grid would further validate.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check: desktop clean · mobile clean. Driven agent matrix on all 5 pages: documentElement scrollWidth-clientWidth = 0 at 1280×800 AND 360×740 (DPR3, touch) — measured overflow=0 on every page/class. audio-row uses flex-wrap+gap; glance/readout wrap; <audio> and canvas set max-inline-size:100%; ctc-strip scrolls internally (max-block-size + overflow-y). CJK transcript uses overflow-wrap:anywhere + word-break:normal + line-break:auto (Japanese wraps between characters, no overflow). Light + dark screenshots read and confirmed on-brand (warm paper / indigo, Georgia display; Japanese legible).
rubric.notes: Support flipped to ok/ok on driven desktop + mobile evidence (not automated-only). Sub-pages share the same CSS + layout patterns.

rubric.evidence: All inference (CTC forward pass + decode) runs in a Web Worker (worker.js) so the ~315M-param model never blocks the main thread; the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned) to the worker. inp-stays-low is manual-evidenced (off-thread architecture). Driven pages stayed responsive with a clean console; the fp32 forward pass took ~11.3 s on WASM for a 12.1 s clip (~1.1× real-time) with the UI responsive throughout.
rubric.notes: An explicit INP measurement during transcription would confirm; the off-thread transfer is the key mitigation.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow wide content scroll
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Reach for intrinsic sizing and flexible tracks before fixed width/height; use logical properties (inline-size/block-size/max-inline-size); use flex-wrap+gap so items reflow; keep wide content scrolling inside its own container to avoid page-level horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — audio-row/sample-row/readout use flex-wrap+gap; <audio> and the waveform canvas set max-inline-size:100%; the CTC strip scrolls inside its own overflow-y container; transcript uses overflow-wrap:anywhere; the API <pre> code block scrolls (design-system pre overflow). Verified overflow=0 at 360×740 and 1280×800 across all 5 pages.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check desktop+mobile clean; driven overflow 0 on every page/class

guidanceConsulted.query: CJK Chinese Japanese text rendering lang attribute line-break word-break
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Mark language with the lang attribute so the browser selects appropriate fonts and line-breaking; let CJK text wrap naturally between characters.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — Japanese content regions (transcript, collapsed, per-frame strip, per-character chips) carry lang="ja" for correct CJK font selection, screen-reader pronunciation, and inter-character line-breaking; the transcript uses word-break:normal + overflow-wrap:anywhere + line-break:auto and a larger font-size/line-height so kanji/kana are legible. No CJK overflow at mobile width.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: driven overflow=0 mobile; light+dark screenshots read (Japanese legible, on-brand)

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates aria-live async loading
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state changes in an accessible live region; give every control an accessible name; keep buttons keyboard-operable.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live=polite loader/status regions; transcript + collapsed use aria-live=polite; per-character replay buttons carry aria-labels; Japanese content marked lang="ja". Driven: 0 unnamed buttons across all 5 pages.

guidanceConsulted.query: run inference off main thread web worker keep INP low
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy/CPU-bound work off the main thread so it doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the CTC forward pass and decode run in models/japanese-xlsr-asr/worker.js; the 16 kHz audio buffer is transferred to the worker. The main thread only draws the waveform and renders the returned strip.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; driven pages stayed responsive; console clean

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible file upload and microphone capture button
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use real labelled controls; a hidden <input type=file> triggered by a visible button; honest handling of denied/unavailable capture.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — Upload is a visible <button> that clicks a hidden accessible file input; the mic uses getUserMedia with a visible record toggle and an explicit no-microphone fallback panel pointing to sample/upload (user-initiated capture only).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: MicRecorder.supported() gate + micFallback panel; upload wired to file input

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied (inherited) — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (shared shell); all pages link the shared stylesheet and crumb back to the catalogue/model page.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: shared design system; crumbs on every page

openQuestions: Drive the full production CDN path end-to-end in a headless run (Download the 1.27 GB fp32 over the HF CDN → ready → Transcribe) — the byte-identical local-mirror run proved the model/features/decode, but the in-harness CDN download exceeds the time budget in this environment; a patient run is queued.

Verify the OPUS-MT ja→en translator (Xenova/opus-mt-ja-en, q8) end-to-end on the multi-model page after both models are cached (same MarianMT path as the built marianmt-translation + spanish-xlsr multi-model demos).
Keyboard-only traversal across all 5 pages: is every control reachable in a sensible focus order with a visible focus ring, and do the per-character replay buttons announce and operate correctly?

Drive the partial/evicted cache and offline-cached states explicitly (delete one cached file → expect Re-download; go offline with a warm cache → expect it to still run) — matters more for the ~1.27 GB fp32 download.
Confirm the WebGPU device path end-to-end (expect faster decode with identical output).

followUpGoals.goal: Add a mobile memory/storage pre-check before the ~1.27 GB fp32 download on constrained devices (this model has no quantized export, so the transfer is unusually large).
Keyboard + screen-reader matrix pass for japanese-xlsr-asr across all 5 pages (focus order, visible focus, SR announcement of the transcript and per-frame grid).
If a genuine q8/q4 Japanese XLSR ONNX with a real CTC head (and a config.json) ever ships, add a lighter variant as a NEW slug so the demo isn't a 1.27 GB download — do not repurpose this fp32 page.

summary: japanese-xlsr-asr is a genuinely-distinct, honest demo: wav2vec2-large-XLSR-53 fine-tuned for Japanese — a cross-lingual CTC recogniser whose output alphabet is a ~2,300-symbol vocab spanning hiragana, katakana and kanji — materially different from the built English wav2vec2, Russian XLSR (Cyrillic), the Latin XLSR demos, and the Mandarin XLSR (a separate character set). Verified runnable on the exact transformers.js 3.7.5 CDN build: real logits [1,605,2341] and a coherent Japanese transcript on the FLEURS (CC-BY 4.0) sample (日本…とされています, with expected character-CTC homophone slips). The FinDIT ONNX-export repo ships only a single self-contained fp32 model.onnx (~1.27 GB, no external-data sidecar) + tokenizer.json — no config.json/preprocessor_config.json — so the worker borrows the architecture config from the base jonatasgrosman repo, normalises audio itself, loads with subfolder:"" + dtype fp32, and CTC-decodes from the ~2,300-entry bundled vocab.json; all disclosed at-a-glance. Driven headless-Chrome matrix on all 5 pages (basics/practical/wild/multi-model): fresh profile shows a labelled ~1.27 GB Download with no silent transfer, overflow=0 desktop+mobile, 0 console errors, 0 failed requests, 0 unnamed buttons; light+dark screenshots read. dtype fp32 chosen honestly (no q8/q4 Japanese XLSR ONNX export exists). Model verified against a byte-identical local mirror because the 1.27 GB fp32 CDN download exceeds the harness time budget (openQuestion for a patient full-CDN run). Per-character forced alignment is the natural fit for space-less Japanese. Revision 1.
